community.definition:
//k(ə)ˈmjunəti//
noun
  1. A group sharing a common understanding, and often the same language, law, manners, and/or tradition.
  2. A residential or religious collective; a commune.
  3. A group of interdependent organisms inhabiting the same region and interacting with each other.
  4. A group of people interacting by electronic means for educational, professional, social, or other purposes; a virtual community.

meanings: A group sharing a common understanding, and often the same language, law, manners, and/or tradition.
A residential or religious collective; a commune.
A group of interdependent organisms inhabiting the same region and interacting with each other.
A group of people interacting by electronic means for educational, professional, social, or other purposes; a virtual community.

community.sentence: Thank you for your interest in contributing to vLLM! Our community is open to everyone and welcomes all kinds of contributions, no matter how small or large. There are several ways you can contribute to the project:

contributions.definition:
//ˌkɒntɹɪˈbjuːʃənz//
noun
  1. Something given or offered that adds to a larger whole.
  2. An amount of money given toward something.
  3. The act of contributing.
  4. The taking part, often with the idea that it has led to (scientific etc.) progress.

meanings: Something given or offered that adds to a larger whole.
An amount of money given toward something.
The act of contributing.
The taking part, often with the idea that it has led to (scientific etc.) progress.

contributions.sentence: Thank you for your interest in contributing to vLLM! Our community is open to everyone and welcomes all kinds of contributions, no matter how small or large. There are several ways you can contribute to the project:

downright.definition:
//ˈdaʊnˌɹaɪt//
adjective
  1. Directed vertically; coming straight down.
  2. Directly to the point; plain
  3. Using plain direct language; accustomed to express opinions directly and bluntly; blunt.
  4. Complete; absolute
adverb
  1. Really; actually; quite
  2. Straight down; perpendicularly.
  3. Plainly, unambiguously; directly.
  4. Without delay; at once.
  e.g. He wasn’t just cool to me, he was downright rude.

meanings: Directed vertically; coming straight down.
Directly to the point; plain
Using plain direct language; accustomed to express opinions directly and bluntly; blunt.
Complete; absolute

definitions.example: He wasn’t just cool to me, he was downright rude.
meanings: Really; actually; quite
Straight down; perpendicularly.
Plainly, unambiguously; directly.
Without delay; at once.

downright.sentence: MkDocs is a fast, simple and downright gorgeous static site generator that's geared towards building project documentation. Documentation source files are written in Markdown, and configured with a single YAML configuration file,  mkdocs.yaml.

environment.definition:
//-mɪnt//
noun
  1. The surroundings of, and influences on, a particular item of interest.
  2. The natural world or ecosystem.
  3. All the elements that affect a system or its inputs and outputs.
  4. A particular political or social setting, arena or condition.

meanings: The surroundings of, and influences on, a particular item of interest.
The natural world or ecosystem.
All the elements that affect a system or its inputs and outputs.
A particular political or social setting, arena or condition.

environment.sentence: It's recommended to use uv, a very fast Python environment manager, to create and manage Python environments. Please follow the documentation to install uv. After installing uv, you can create a new Python environment using the following commands:

following.definition:
//ˈfɒləʊɪŋ//
verb
  1. To go after; to pursue; to move behind in the same path or direction.
  2. To go or come after in a sequence.
  3. To carry out (orders, instructions, etc.).
  4. To live one's life according to (religion, teachings, etc).
  e.g. Follow that car!
noun
  1. A group of followers, attendants or admirers; an entourage.
  2. Vocation; business; profession.
  3. (with definite article, treated as singular or plural) A thing or things to be mentioned immediately after.
  e.g. He had a loyal following.
adjective
  1. Coming next, either in sequence or in time.
  2. About to be specified.
  3. (of a wind) Blowing in the direction of travel.
  e.g. See the following section.
preposition
  1. After, subsequent to.
  e.g. Following the meeting, we all had a chat.

meanings: To go after; to pursue; to move behind in the same path or direction.
To go or come after in a sequence.
To carry out (orders, instructions, etc.).
To live one's life according to (religion, teachings, etc).
A group of followers, attendants or admirers; an entourage.
Vocation; business; profession.
(with definite article, treated as singular or plural) A thing or things to be mentioned immediately after.
Coming next, either in sequence or in time.
About to be specified.
(of a wind) Blowing in the direction of travel.

definitions.example: Following the meeting, we all had a chat.
meanings: After, subsequent to.

following.sentence: Unsure on where to start? Check out the following links for tasks to work on:

format.definition:
//ˈfɔː(ɹ).mæt//
noun
  1. The layout of a publication or document.
  2. (hence) The form of presentation of something.
  3. The type of programming that a radio station broadcasts; such as a certain genre of music, news, sports, talk, etc.
  4. A file type.
verb
  1. To create or edit the layout of a document.
  2. Change a document so it will fit onto a different type of page.
  3. To prepare a mass storage medium for initial use, erasing any existing data in the process.

meanings: The layout of a publication or document.
(hence) The form of presentation of something.
The type of programming that a radio station broadcasts; such as a certain genre of music, news, sports, talk, etc.
A file type.
To create or edit the layout of a document.
Change a document so it will fit onto a different type of page.
To prepare a mass storage medium for initial use, erasing any existing data in the process.

gorgeous.definition:
//ˈɡɔːdʒəs//
adjective
  1. (of a person or place) Very beautiful.
  2. Very enjoyable, pleasant, tasty, etc.
  e.g. All the contest judges agreed that Brigitt was absolutely gorgeous.

definitions.example: All the contest judges agreed that Brigitt was absolutely gorgeous.
meanings: (of a person or place) Very beautiful.
Very enjoyable, pleasant, tasty, etc.

gorgeous.sentence: MkDocs is a fast, simple and downright gorgeous static site generator that's geared towards building project documentation. Documentation source files are written in Markdown, and configured with a single YAML configuration file,  mkdocs.yaml.

maintain.definition:
//meɪnˈteɪn//
verb
  1. To support (someone), to back up or assist (someone) in an action.
  2. To keep up; to preserve; to uphold (a state, condition etc.).
  3. To declare or affirm (a clause) to be true; to assert.

meanings: To support (someone), to back up or assist (someone) in an action.
To keep up; to preserve; to uphold (a state, condition etc.).
To declare or affirm (a clause) to be true; to assert.

maintain.sentence: Thank you for your contribution to vLLM! Before submitting the pull request, please ensure the PR meets the following criteria. This helps vLLM maintain the code quality and improve the efficiency of the review process.

necessary.definition:
//ˈnɛsəsɹɪ//
noun
  1. (usually with the definite article) A place to do the "necessary" business of urination and defecation: an outhouse or lavatory.
adjective
  1. Required, essential, whether logically inescapable or needed in order to achieve a desired result or avoid some penalty.
  2. Unavoidable, inevitable.
  3. Determined, involuntary: acting from compulsion rather than free will.
  e.g. Although I wished to think that all was false, it was yet necessary that I, who thus thought, must in some sense exist.

meanings: (usually with the definite article) A place to do the "necessary" business of urination and defecation: an outhouse or lavatory.

definitions.example: Although I wished to think that all was false, it was yet necessary that I, who thus thought, must in some sense exist.
meanings: Required, essential, whether logically inescapable or needed in order to achieve a desired result or avoid some penalty.
Unavoidable, inevitable.
Determined, involuntary: acting from compulsion rather than free will.

necessary.sentence: Then install the necessary build dependencies from requirements/build/cuda.txt, skipping torch as it was installed in the previous step:

recommendations.definition:
noun
  1. An act of recommending.
  2. That which is recommended.
  3. A commendation or endorsement.
  4. A suggestion or proposal about the best course of action (with adpositions including "about" for the context and "to" for the course of action)

meanings: An act of recommending.
That which is recommended.
A commendation or endorsement.
A suggestion or proposal about the best course of action (with adpositions including "about" for the context and "to" for the course of action)

recommendations.sentence: For an optimized workflow when iterating on C++/CUDA kernels, see the Incremental Compilation Workflow for recommendations.

recommended.definition:
//ˌɹɛkəˈmɛndɪd//
verb
  1. To bestow commendation on; to represent favourably; to suggest, endorse or encourage as an appropriate choice.
  2. To make acceptable; to attract favor to.
  3. To advise, propose, counsel favorably
  4. To commit, confide to another's care, confidence or acceptance, with favoring representations
  e.g. The board recommends Philips, given his ample experience in similar positions.

definitions.example: The board recommends Philips, given his ample experience in similar positions.
meanings: To bestow commendation on; to represent favourably; to suggest, endorse or encourage as an appropriate choice.
To make acceptable; to attract favor to.
To advise, propose, counsel favorably
To commit, confide to another's care, confidence or acceptance, with favoring representations

snippet.definition:
//ˈsnɪpɪt//
noun
  1. A small part of something, such as a song or fabric; sample.
  2. A text file containing a relatively small amount of code, useless by itself, along with instructions for inserting that code into a larger codebase.
  e.g. From the snippet I heard of their rehearsal, they sound pretty good.
verb
  1. To produce a snippet (small part) of; to excerpt.
  2. To make small cuts, to snip, particularly with scissors.
  e.g. We snippeted the blog posts for display on the home page.

definitions.example: From the snippet I heard of their rehearsal, they sound pretty good.
meanings: A small part of something, such as a song or fabric; sample.
A text file containing a relatively small amount of code, useless by itself, along with instructions for inserting that code into a larger codebase.

definitions.example: We snippeted the blog posts for display on the home page.
meanings: To produce a snippet (small part) of; to excerpt.
To make small cuts, to snip, particularly with scissors.
